title: Sarah Champion MP Visits to Witness Cutting-Edge Manufacturing Innovation
author: Productive Machines
intro: Sarah Champion, Member of Parliament for Rotherham, visited Productive Machines to learn about the company's transformative impact on UK manufacturing and experience SenseNC™ firsthand.

children.text: On a bright July morning, Sarah Champion MP joined the Productive Machines team at our headquarters in the Advanced Manufacturing Park, Rotherham. The visit reflected her continued commitment to championing innovation, advanced manufacturing and high-skilled jobs across South Yorkshire.
Cutting-edge software, in plain sight

Sarah was welcomed by CEO and co-founder Dr Erdem Ozturk, who walked her through the live SenseNC™ platform. The MP saw how UK-based shops are using the same physics models pioneered at the AMRC to deliver 53% cycle-time reductions on real aerospace parts.
"It is brilliant to see a UK spin-out turning world-class research into measurable wins for British manufacturing." — Sarah Champion MP
Why the visit mattered

UK manufacturing competitiveness depends on bridging the gap between research and shop-floor adoption. Productive Machines was founded specifically to close that gap — bringing AMRC-grade machining science to every CNC shop without requiring a research budget or a PhD on staff.
• Over 200 CNC machines now running SenseNC across the UK
• Average customer reports +110% productivity uplift in year one
• UKISF Manufacturing Innovation award winner, 2023
What's next

Following the visit, Sarah expressed strong support for continued government-industry collaboration on advanced manufacturing. We look forward to welcoming more public-sector partners and demonstrating, with hard numbers, what UK-built software is delivering on real shop floors today.

title: Innovations in Aerospace Manufacturing — Key Takeaways from MIC 2025
author: Dr Cristina Sesma-Forcada
intro: The 24th Machining Innovations Conference highlighted aerospace manufacturing advancements in sustainability, digitalisation, and AI, featuring expert talks, live demos, and exhibitions.

children.text: The Machining Innovations Conference is the most important annual gathering for the machining research community. MIC 2025, held in Hannover in October, was no exception — and this year the centre of gravity shifted unmistakably towards real-time intelligence on the shop floor.
Three themes dominated
Across the three-day programme, three threads came up again and again:
• Sustainability through optimisation — reducing scrap and rework, not just energy
• Digital twins moving from labs to production
• AI as augmentation, not replacement for skilled programmers
Our keynote on chatter

Prof Erdem Ozturk delivered the opening keynote on the latest stability-lobe research powering SenseNC Finesse. The headline result: a new ML-augmented model that predicts cut stability with under 2% deviation from physical measurement, across every tested aerospace material.
"The era of trial-and-error machining is genuinely ending. The question now is how fast the industry adopts what's already working." — Prof Erdem Ozturk, keynote
Live demo at the AMRC booth

We ran live SenseNC demos throughout the week — visitors saw a Ti-6Al-4V impeller optimised end-to-end on a Siemens NX terminal. The session that attracted the most engagement: a side-by-side comparison of a SenseNC-optimised toolpath versus the original Mastercam programme on the same machine.
What we took home
• Surface-finish prediction is the next frontier — and we have a strong head start
• The OEM appetite for native integrations is growing — expect more announcements through 2026
• Customers want audited gains, not promises — our dashboard work is paying off
Next stop: MACH 2026 in Birmingham — come find us at the AMRC stand for live demos and a free tap test on your tool.

Three themes dominated

Across the three-day programme, three threads came up again and again:

  1. Sustainability through optimisation — reducing scrap and rework, not just energy
  2. Digital twins moving from labs to production
  3. AI as augmentation, not replacement for skilled programmers

Our keynote on chatter

Prof Erdem Ozturk delivered the opening keynote on the latest stability-lobe research powering SenseNC Finesse. The headline result: a new ML-augmented model that predicts cut stability with under 2% deviation from physical measurement, across every tested aerospace material.

"The era of trial-and-error machining is genuinely ending. The question now is how fast the industry adopts what's already working." — Prof Erdem Ozturk, keynote

Live demo at the AMRC booth

We ran live SenseNC demos throughout the week — visitors saw a Ti-6Al-4V impeller optimised end-to-end on a Siemens NX terminal. The session that attracted the most engagement: a side-by-side comparison of a SenseNC-optimised toolpath versus the original Mastercam programme on the same machine.

What we took home

title: AML's Success with Productive Machines
author: Productive Machines
intro: By maximising the efficiency of machine tools, Productive Machines empowers machinists to achieve superior quality parts faster and with greater precision.

children.text: AML Aerospace, a precision-machining supplier based in the UK, faced a familiar challenge: cycle times on titanium aerospace components were eating into margins, and chatter vibrations were limiting how aggressively their programmers could push.
The starting point

Like most aerospace shops, AML had been managing chatter through conservative cuts and safety margins. The result was reliable parts — but cycles that ran 30–40% longer than the machine's true envelope allowed.
• Fleet: 12 five-axis CNC machines
• Primary material: Ti-6Al-4V, Inconel
• Average part: turbine impeller, fan blades
The trial

AML's engineering team agreed to a four-week SenseNC Finesse pilot, focused on a single high-volume Ti-6Al-4V impeller program. Our application engineer, Dr Vahid Vajdi, ran tap testing on three machines and built physics-accurate twins for each. The optimised programme was loaded back into NX CAM with full audit trails.

"Innovation is part of AML's DNA, we are constantly looking at innovative ways to improve our manufacturing processes and reduce the carbon footprint. Productive Machines ticked all the boxes and helped us become more competitive." — Dr Gareth Morgan, Managing Director, AML
The results — measured, not estimated
• −53% cycle time on the target part (42:18 → 19:52)
• +38% tool life across all carbide endmills used
• 0 chatter events recorded over 200+ optimised cuts
• 11% lower tooling spend in the first full quarter post-deployment
What happened next

Three months after the pilot, SenseNC Finesse is AML's default workflow on every 5-axis aerospace job. The deployment paid for itself in under six weeks, and AML's programming team has expanded the workflow to titanium fan blades and Inconel blisks.
This is precisely the story we tell every prospective customer: send us a real part. We will benchmark it on your CAM file. The numbers will speak for themselves.

headline: AML cut titanium impeller cycle time by 53% — and it stuck.
intro: A four-week pilot turned into AML's default workflow on every 5-axis job. Here's exactly what changed, in their numbers.
quote: Innovation is part of AML's DNA, we are constantly looking at innovative ways to improve our manufacturing processes and reduce the carbon footprint. Productive Machines ticked all the boxes and helped us become more competitive.

sections.title: The challenge

children.text: Cycle times on titanium aerospace components were eating into margins. Chatter vibrations on Ti-6Al-4V impellers forced AML's programmers to drop feed rates and add safety margins — reliable parts, but cycles 30–40% longer than the machine's true envelope.
The 5-axis fleet was healthy. The CAM programmers were experienced. The bottleneck was knowing exactly how hard the machines could be pushed without producing scrap.

sections.legacyHtml:
Cycle times on titanium aerospace components were eating into margins. Chatter vibrations on Ti-6Al-4V impellers forced AML's programmers to drop feed rates and add safety margins — reliable parts, but cycles 30–40% longer than the machine's true envelope.

The 5-axis fleet was healthy. The CAM programmers were experienced. The bottleneck was knowing exactly how hard the machines could be pushed without producing scrap.

sections.title: The SenseNC approach

children.text: A four-week SenseNC Finesse pilot focused on a single high-volume Ti-6Al-4V impeller. Our application engineer ran tap testing on three machines and built physics-accurate twins for each. The optimised program loaded back into NX CAM with full audit trails.
What we did
• Tap-tested 3 five-axis machines (under 90 seconds each)
• Built physics-accurate digital twins per spindle / holder / tool combination
• Optimised every move in the toolpath
• Validated cuts on AMRC-equivalent machines before shipping back

name: TapStarter — instant spindle speeds, no CAM required.
intro: The fastest way to optimal feeds and speeds. Upload a tool combo. Get a safe operating range in seconds. Perfect for shops without a CAM seat — or as a sanity check before any job.

sections.title: What is TapStarter?

children.text: TapStarter is our entry-tier web app for any machine shop that needs reliable spindle speeds and feed rates — fast.
No installation. No CAM swap. No training. Open the app, enter your spindle, tool and material, and TapStarter gives you a verified operating range backed by AMRC physics models.

It's the perfect starting point if you want to feel SenseNC's value before committing to a full CAM integration. Many of our customers begin with TapStarter and graduate to SenseNC Feeds within a quarter.

sections.legacyHtml:
TapStarter is our entry-tier web app for any machine shop that needs reliable spindle speeds and feed rates — fast.

No installation. No CAM swap. No training. Open the app, enter your spindle, tool and material, and TapStarter gives you a verified operating range backed by AMRC physics models.

It's the perfect starting point if you want to feel SenseNC's value before committing to a full CAM integration. Many of our customers begin with TapStarter and graduate to SenseNC Feeds within a quarter.

sections.title: How it works

children.text: 1. Open the web app
No download, no install. Works on any modern browser, on any device — including the tablet next to your machine.
2. Enter your setup
Spindle make and model, tool diameter and length, holder, material. Optional tap test for a precise digital twin.
3. Get optimal feeds & speeds
TapStarter returns a safe operating range — RPM, feed, depth of cut and stability margin — that respects your machine's true envelope.

3. Get optimal feeds & speeds

TapStarter returns a safe operating range — RPM, feed, depth of cut and stability margin — that respects your machine's true envelope.

• AMRC-validated — same physics engine as our enterprise tier

name: SenseNC Feeds — toolpath optimisation inside your CAM.
intro: Plug SenseNC directly into Siemens NX or Mastercam. Optimised feeds and speeds for every move in your program — without changing the way your programmers work.

sections.title: What is SenseNC Feeds?

children.text: SenseNC Feeds is our CAM-integrated feed-rate optimisation tool. It plugs into Siemens NX and Mastercam and sends every move in your program at the optimal feed.
The difference shows up immediately: faster cycles, longer tool life, cleaner surface finishes. And because it runs inside your existing CAM, your programmers stay productive on day one.

sections.legacyHtml:
SenseNC Feeds is our CAM-integrated feed-rate optimisation tool. It plugs into Siemens NX and Mastercam and sends every move in your program at the optimal feed.

The difference shows up immediately: faster cycles, longer tool life, cleaner surface finishes. And because it runs inside your existing CAM, your programmers stay productive on day one.

sections.title: Native CAM integration

children.text: SenseNC Feeds installs as a plugin in your CAM environment — no separate license server, no file export ceremony, no workflow change.
Siemens NX
Adds an "Optimise feeds" panel to the operation view. Click optimise, and SenseNC analyses every toolpath move using your machine's verified physics model.
Mastercam
Lives as a chook accessible from the CAM toolbar. Same one-click optimisation, with output to the operation's feed override values.

sections.legacyHtml:
SenseNC Feeds installs as a plugin in your CAM environment — no separate license server, no file export ceremony, no workflow change.

Siemens NX

Adds an "Optimise feeds" panel to the operation view. Click optimise, and SenseNC analyses every toolpath move using your machine's verified physics model.

Mastercam

Lives as a chook accessible from the CAM toolbar. Same one-click optimisation, with output to the operation's feed override values.

sections.title: AMRC-verified physics

children.text: Every optimisation runs through our digital-twin model — validated against thousands of recorded cuts at the AMRC at Sheffield.
• Spindle response modelling per make/model
• Tool-holder dynamics (HSK, BT, Capto)
• Material removal rate vs. surface finish trade-offs
• Live stability lobes for each tool

sections.legacyHtml:
Every optimisation runs through our digital-twin model — validated against thousands of recorded cuts at the AMRC at Sheffield.

  • Spindle response modelling per make/model
  • Tool-holder dynamics (HSK, BT, Capto)
  • Material removal rate vs. surface finish trade-offs
  • Live stability lobes for each tool

• 40–50% cycle time reduction on 5-axis work
• 38% longer average tool life
• 11% reduction in tooling spend
• Payback in under 3 months on most fleets

name: SenseNC Finesse — digital twin. Zero chatter.
intro: The complete SenseNC platform — full toolpath optimisation, real-time chatter elimination, in-process monitoring. Used by Boeing, Siemens and AML Aerospace.

sections.title: The complete platform

children.text: SenseNC Finesse is our flagship product. It combines everything in SenseNC Feeds with full digital-twin simulation, real-time chatter elimination and in-process monitoring.
For aerospace, energy and high-precision manufacturers, Finesse isn't an upgrade — it's the difference between hitting your delivery commitments and missing them.

sections.legacyHtml:
SenseNC Finesse is our flagship product. It combines everything in SenseNC Feeds with full digital-twin simulation, real-time chatter elimination and in-process monitoring.

For aerospace, energy and high-precision manufacturers, Finesse isn't an upgrade — it's the difference between hitting your delivery commitments and missing them.

sections.title: Digital twin simulation

children.text: Finesse builds a physics-accurate digital twin of every machine, spindle, tool and holder in your fleet. The twin runs alongside the real machine, predicting chatter before it happens.
• Live stability lobes per tool
• Spindle frequency response per machine
• Multi-axis force prediction
• Surface finish forecasting

sections.legacyHtml:
Finesse builds a physics-accurate digital twin of every machine, spindle, tool and holder in your fleet. The twin runs alongside the real machine, predicting chatter before it happens.

  • Live stability lobes per tool
  • Spindle frequency response per machine
  • Multi-axis force prediction
  • Surface finish forecasting

sections.title: Real-time chatter elimination

children.text: Where Feeds optimises before the cut, Finesse intervenes during it. Sensors on the spindle (or feed-rate overrides via Machine Tool Interface) keep every cut inside the stability envelope.
"We eliminated chatter on our titanium aerospace parts and cut cycle time by 53%. That was the deciding moment for our fleet roll-out." — Dr Gareth Morgan, AML Aerospace

Where Feeds optimises before the cut, Finesse intervenes during it. Sensors on the spindle (or feed-rate overrides via Machine Tool Interface) keep every cut inside the stability envelope.

"We eliminated chatter on our titanium aerospace parts and cut cycle time by 53%. That was the deciding moment for our fleet roll-out." — Dr Gareth Morgan, AML Aerospace

• Run on-site tap testing and validation
• Train your programmers and machinists
• Provide ongoing optimisation reviews (monthly or quarterly)

seo.title: SenseNC Finesse — digital twin. Zero chatter.
seo.description: The complete SenseNC platform — full toolpath optimisation, real-time chatter elimination, in-process monitoring. Used by Boeing, Siemens and AML Aerospace.

title: From AMRC bench to 200+ shop floors.
intro: A decade of chatter-vibration research. A spin-out in 2019. A platform now running across aerospace, motorsport and precision manufacturing worldwide.

sections.title: It started at AMRC

children.text: The Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre (AMRC) at the University of Sheffield has been the UK's home of chatter-vibration research for nearly two decades. Professors Erdem Ozturk and his team published the foundational work on stability lobes that the wider industry now relies on.
By 2018 the research had produced a tool good enough that aerospace and motorsport partners were asking for production access. The university spun the team out as Productive Machines in 2019.

sections.legacyHtml:
The Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre (AMRC) at the University of Sheffield has been the UK's home of chatter-vibration research for nearly two decades. Professors Erdem Ozturk and his team published the foundational work on stability lobes that the wider industry now relies on.

By 2018 the research had produced a tool good enough that aerospace and motorsport partners were asking for production access. The university spun the team out as Productive Machines in 2019.

sections.title: Milestones

children.text: 2019 — Spin-out
Founded by Dr Cristina Sesma alongside the AMRC research team. First UK Innovation Programme grant secured.
2021 — First customers
SenseNC Feeds ships to Boeing and Siemens partner programmes. AMRC validation completed across 14 machine tools.
2023 — Award recognition
UKISF Manufacturing Innovation award. 50+ live machines across the UK and EU.
2025 — NX integration
Siemens publishes the official NX CAM integration. Over 200 deployments worldwide.

sections.legacyHtml:
2019 — Spin-out

Founded by Dr Cristina Sesma alongside the AMRC research team. First UK Innovation Programme grant secured.

2021 — First customers

SenseNC Feeds ships to Boeing and Siemens partner programmes. AMRC validation completed across 14 machine tools.

2023 — Award recognition

UKISF Manufacturing Innovation award. 50+ live machines across the UK and EU.

2025 — NX integration

Siemens publishes the official NX CAM integration. Over 200 deployments worldwide.

sections.title: Where we are now

children.text: Today, Productive Machines runs on more than 200 CNC machines across aerospace, motorsport, energy and medical manufacturing. The team has grown from four founders to 24 engineers, machinists and customer-success specialists. We're still based at the Advanced Manufacturing Park in Rotherham, less than a mile from the AMRC labs where the research started.

title: Best part. Faster. First time.
intro: Every CNC operator should have an expert on their shoulder — telling them exactly how hard to push, every cut, every job.

sections.title: Our promise

children.text: We make one promise to every customer: the best part, faster, first time. Every brand decision — colour, word, component, animation — should reinforce this promise. If it doesn't serve the machine shop, it doesn't belong.
"The chatter is not the operator's fault. The machine has limits — most operators just have no way of knowing exactly where those limits are. We give them eyes." — Dr Cristina Sesma, CEO

sections.title: How we work

children.text: Confident, never hedging
"Eliminates chatter" — not "may help reduce". Every claim backed by a verified stat or case study.
Outcome-first
Lead with results. "53% faster cycle times." Features are the reason for the result — not the headline.
Expert & human
Use jargon in context. Always connect technical features to a clear business outcome your reader cares about.

sections.title: Where we're going

children.text: By 2030 we want SenseNC to be the default — not a competitive advantage — for serious CNC machining. That means:
• Native integration with every major CAM platform
• A physics model for every commercial spindle, tool and holder on the market
• Real-time chatter elimination on machines worth £30k as well as machines worth £3m
• An open ecosystem so machinists, tooling vendors and CAM houses can all build on the same foundation

title: Physics + ML — productised.
intro: A decade of chatter-vibration research, packaged so any programmer can run it. Here's how SenseNC actually works.

sections.title: The physics

children.text: Chatter is a self-excited vibration phenomenon — the cutting force varies as the tool engages and disengages the workpiece, which feeds back into spindle motion, which changes the cutting force again.
The maths is well-understood — the challenge is solving it fast enough, for enough machines, to be useful on a shop floor. That's what we've built.

sections.legacyHtml:
Chatter is a self-excited vibration phenomenon — the cutting force varies as the tool engages and disengages the workpiece, which feeds back into spindle motion, which changes the cutting force again.

The maths is well-understood — the challenge is solving it fast enough, for enough machines, to be useful on a shop floor. That's what we've built.

sections.title: The digital twin

children.text: For every machine in your fleet, we maintain a physics-accurate digital twin: spindle frequency response, holder dynamics, tool stick-out, workpiece fixturing. The twin runs alongside the real machine, predicting stability lobes for every operation.
• Spindle frequency response: measured via tap test or modelled from machine class
• Tool dynamics: per-holder, per-stick-out, per-material
• Workpiece compliance: factored into stability lobe calculation

sections.legacyHtml:
For every machine in your fleet, we maintain a physics-accurate digital twin: spindle frequency response, holder dynamics, tool stick-out, workpiece fixturing. The twin runs alongside the real machine, predicting stability lobes for every operation.

  • Spindle frequency response: measured via tap test or modelled from machine class
  • Tool dynamics: per-holder, per-stick-out, per-material
  • Workpiece compliance: factored into stability lobe calculation

sections.title: The machine learning layer

children.text: Where pure physics struggles — surface finish prediction, tool wear, multi-axis interference — we use ML models trained on thousands of recorded cuts at AMRC. Together with the physics core, this is what makes SenseNC accurate beyond textbook chatter prediction.

sections.legacyHtml: Where pure physics struggles — surface finish prediction, tool wear, multi-axis interference — we use ML models trained on thousands of recorded cuts at AMRC. Together with the physics core, this is what makes SenseNC accurate beyond textbook chatter prediction.

sections.title: Compute

children.text: SenseNC runs millions of stability-lobe calculations per program. We use high-performance computing to do this in parallel — what used to take days of researcher time now ships in minutes inside your CAM.

sections.legacyHtml: SenseNC runs millions of stability-lobe calculations per program. We use high-performance computing to do this in parallel — what used to take days of researcher time now ships in minutes inside your CAM.

Tomas Petrov — Head of Engineering. 15 years at Siemens PLM. Now leads the SenseNC product team.

sections.title: How we work

children.text: Engineers first
Tiny PR-to-merge cycles. No design-by-committee. Every engineer is expected to talk to customers and visit shop floors.
Built at AMRC
We're still embedded in the AMRC ecosystem. Research flows in, products flow out, customer problems flow back to research.
Hybrid by default
Most of the team is in Rotherham 2–3 days a week. We support remote work for the rest — though we expect everyone in person for major releases and customer visits.

title: Resell SenseNC across your region.
intro: A growing global network of channel partners taking SenseNC to local machine shops with full Productive Machines support — training, co-marketing, technical handoff and revenue share.

sections.title: Programme overview

children.text: The Productive Machines Channel Partner programme is built for resellers, distributors, integrators and consultants who serve the global CNC machining market. We offer three tiers — Silver, Gold and Platinum — with progressively richer support and margin as you grow.

sections.legacyHtml: The Productive Machines Channel Partner programme is built for resellers, distributors, integrators and consultants who serve the global CNC machining market. We offer three tiers — Silver, Gold and Platinum — with progressively richer support and margin as you grow.

sections.title: The three tiers

children.text: Silver — getting started
For first-year partners. 20% revenue share. Co-marketing kit, sales enablement, 1 product certification.
Gold — established
£250k+ booked. 30% revenue share. Lead share, joint webinars, 3 product certifications, named partner manager.
Platinum — strategic
£1M+ booked or strategic territory. 40% revenue share. Full product roadmap influence, joint engineering, co-marketing budget, exclusive territory option.

title: SenseNC inside the CAM you already use.
intro: Native plugins for Siemens NX and Mastercam — with Fusion 360, hyperMILL and SolidCAM in development. SenseNC sits where your programmers already work.

sections.title: Siemens NX

children.text: SenseNC ships as a first-class plugin inside Siemens NX CAM, launched in 2025. The integration adds an "Optimise feeds" command to every operation — one click sends the toolpath to SenseNC, gets the optimised feeds back, and updates the operation in place.
• Available NX 2406 and later
• No file export — direct API integration
• Operation-level or program-level optimisation
• Full audit trail per cut

sections.legacyHtml:
SenseNC ships as a first-class plugin inside Siemens NX CAM, launched in 2025. The integration adds an "Optimise feeds" command to every operation — one click sends the toolpath to SenseNC, gets the optimised feeds back, and updates the operation in place.

  • Available NX 2406 and later
  • No file export — direct API integration
  • Operation-level or program-level optimisation
  • Full audit trail per cut

sections.title: Mastercam

children.text: SenseNC Feeds runs as a chook accessible from the Mastercam toolbar. Same one-click optimisation as NX, with output to the operation's feed override values.
• Mastercam 2024 and later
• Toolpath, operation or feature-level optimisation
• Multi-machine routing supported

sections.legacyHtml:
SenseNC Feeds runs as a chook accessible from the Mastercam toolbar. Same one-click optimisation as NX, with output to the operation's feed override values.

  • Mastercam 2024 and later
  • Toolpath, operation or feature-level optimisation
  • Multi-machine routing supported

sections.title: AMRC Sheffield

children.text: The Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre (AMRC) at the University of Sheffield is where the research that became SenseNC started, and remains our closest research partner. Every quarter we publish updates from the AMRC team back into the product.

• 14 industrial machine tools used for validation
• Joint PhD programme — 4 students currently active
